# Figure description: Картограмма_бурения.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Картограмма_бурения.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **index map / coverage diagram** (схема) showing the spatial distribution of drilling works (буровых работ) conducted by third-party (сторонних) organizations whose materials were incorporated into the report. It is a schematic grid map overlain with annotated polygons representing the areal coverage of prior survey reports by different authors and years.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a multi-sheet area spanning **map sheets XXXII through XXXV** (top row) and **sheets II through XVII** (lower rows), corresponding to 1:200,000-scale nomenclature sheets in the **M-42 and N-42** series of northern Kazakhstan. The grid divides the study area into numbered quadrangle cells. The compass indicator in the upper right confirms standard north orientation (marked **N/M**).

Named geographic or administrative zones are not explicitly labeled, but the sheet numbers correspond to the regional survey area described in Report 36572.

---

## Key Content

The map uses **differently hatched and patterned polygons** (at least 8–10 distinct fill patterns) to distinguish the areal coverage zones of different prior drilling campaigns conducted by various geologists/organizations. Each polygon is annotated with the **author's surname and year** of the respective report. The spatial arrangement shows:

- **Dense clustering of overlapping survey polygons** in the central portion of the grid (sheets III, VIII–IX, XIV–XV), indicating this zone has the highest concentration of prior drilling data.
- **Sparser coverage** toward the periphery — sheets XXXII (upper left), IV–V (upper right), X–XI (middle right), XIII, XVI–XVII (lower right) — showing little or no prior drilling data incorporated.
- The **upper zone** (sheets XXXII–XXXV, along the N-42/M-42 boundary) has moderate coverage with several named surveys.
- Different **hatch patterns** (diagonal lines at various angles, dotted patterns, wavy lines, cross-hatching, triangular symbols, dashed outlines) represent different organizations or report series, functioning as a de facto legend through pattern differentiation, though no formal legend box is present on the figure.

## Research Value

This figure provides a **comprehensive bibliographic and spatial inventory** of all prior drilling campaigns whose data were synthesized into Report 36572, allowing a geologist or research assistant to immediately identify which sub-areas have robust pre-existing borehole control versus data gaps. It reveals that the **central sheets (III, VIII–IX, XIV–XV)** are the most data-rich zones with overlapping multi-decade drilling histories (1950s–1980s), while peripheral sheets remain essentially unconstrained by legacy drilling data. For mineral potential assessment, this map is critical for evaluating **data reliability and spatial confidence** in subsurface interpretations — areas with dense prior coverage support more robust geological modeling, whereas peripheral zones require additional investigation.
